headline: Freshly exposed backdoors—from a Southeast Asian espionage toolkit to a ransomware-feeding access broker—dominated the day as police across Europe and Asia dismantled SIM-swap and piracy operations.

emerging_trends.text: Several state-aligned crews surfaced new implants in a single day, with Russia's FSB-run Gamaredon refining its loaders and server concealment and the CL-STA-1062 operation deploying the custom TinyRCT backdoor against Southeast Asian governments.
Police forces booked wins against cybercrime, from Poland's arrest of a SIM-swap gang behind millions in crypto theft to the seizure of 44 PirloTV piracy domains.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Police Hit SIM-Swap and Piracy Operations

emerging_trends.text: Trusted software channels keep doubling as attack paths, exemplified by a 10-million-install Chrome ad blocker found hiding the ability to inject arbitrary JavaScript.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Chrome Ad Blocker Hides Script Injection

topics.title: State-Sponsored Espionage Sharpens Its Backdoors
topics.latest_developments: Unit 42 detailed CL-STA-1062, a hybrid toolkit built around the custom TinyRCT backdoor that struck Southeast Asian governments and critical infrastructure; Microsoft Threat Intelligence found a photo-themed ZIP campaign planting a persistent Node.js implant in European and Asian hospitality firms; and Dark Reading reported that Russia's FSB-run Gamaredon improved its malware loading and server hiding.
topics.summary: Three espionage operations surfaced at once, each pairing custom implants with stealth tradecraft. Defenders facing CL-STA-1062, the hospitality Node.js implant, or Gamaredon should hunt for unusual loaders, fake image shortcut files, and beaconing to concealed infrastructure.

sources.title: CL-STA-1062 Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure
Photo ZIP campaign targeting hospitality industry delivers Node.js implant for persistent access
Russian APT 'Gamaredon' Upgrades Its Arsenal, Requiring New Defenses

topics.title: Russia-Ukraine Cyber Front Widens
topics.latest_developments: A dairy manufacturer in Russia's republic of Bashkortostan became the latest food producer there to have operations snarled by a cyberattack; Ukraine's state-owned postal operator reported app disruptions from a suspected attack; and researchers found Russia kept using Cellebrite's phone-cracking tool against a dissident years after the firm said in March 2021 it would stop serving the country.
topics.summary: The war's cyber dimension keeps disrupting civilian infrastructure on both sides while Russian authorities repurpose Western forensic tools for repression. Organizations in the conflict zone should brace for service-disrupting intrusions, and surveillance vendors face fresh questions about controlling deployed gear.

sources.title: Another Russian dairy company reportedly disrupted by cyberattack
Ukraine's state postal operator reports app disruption after cyberattack
Russia used Cellebrite phone-hacking tool to crack down on dissident after firm cut off country

topics.title: Police Hit SIM-Swap and Piracy Operations
topics.latest_developments: Polish authorities arrested four members of a cybercrime group that breached telecom partners to run SIM-swapping attacks tied to millions in cryptocurrency theft; investigators seized 44 domains linked to the PirloTV sports-piracy network; and Dark Reading reported that local police collusion keeps Southeast Asian scam centers running despite international pressure.
topics.summary: Law enforcement landed fresh blows against telecom-enabled fraud and illegal streaming, while entrenched corruption still shields the multibillion-dollar scam-center economy. Telecom carriers should tighten SIM-change controls and account-recovery verification.

sources.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
PirloTV sports piracy network disrupted as 44 domains seized
Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers

topics.title: CISA Plans Hiring as Windows 10 Support Extends
topics.latest_developments: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin told lawmakers that President Trump has met a potential CISA director nominee and that the agency will hire 600 once a director is seated; Microsoft quietly extended free Windows 10 Extended Security Updates for consumers to October 12, 2027; and NIST opened updated IoT security guidance for public review.
topics.summary: U.S. policy moves touched the federal cyber workforce, legacy-operating-system support, and device security baselines. Enterprises still running Windows 10 gain another year of consumer ESU coverage, and IoT vendors can comment on the draft federal requirements.

sources.title: DHS chief says president has met with potential CISA nominee; agency plans to hire 600
Microsoft quietly extends free Windows 10 ESU support to October 2027
NIST Opens Updated IoT Security Guidance to Public Review

topics.title: Mistic Backdoor Feeds Ransomware Crews
topics.latest_developments: Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team tied the new Mistic backdoor, also called MLTBackdoor, to the initial access broker Woodgnat, known as KongTuke, which has fed ransomware operations including Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, and 8Base, with the implant hitting insurance, education, IT, and professional-services firms since April 2026.
topics.summary: Mistic is a stealthy backdoor spread through ClickFix and ModeloRAT campaigns by a financially motivated access broker active since May 2024. Affected sectors should watch for KongTuke lures that precede ransomware deployment.

sources.title: Stealthy new backdoor surfaces in attacks on multiple sectors
New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns

topics.title: Chrome Ad Blocker Hides Script Injection
topics.latest_developments: Island researchers found that Adblock for YouTube, a Chrome extension carrying more than 10 million installs and a Featured badge on the Chrome Web Store, holds dormant code able to execute arbitrary JavaScript on pages users visit.
topics.summary: The extension, ID cmedhionkhpnakcndndgjdbohmhepckk, advertises ad blocking yet conceals script-injection capability that could hijack browsing sessions. Users should remove it and audit their installed extensions.

sources.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability

games.recap: Ashcraft, Rodriguez, O'Hearn help Pirates rout AL West-leading Mariners 11-1
Brandon Lowe, Henry Davis each homer to lead Pirates over Mariners 5-1

business_politics.title: AI Memory Shortage Jolts Tech Stocks
business_politics.latest_developments: Apple raised MacBook and iPad prices 20% on June 25, blaming an AI-driven memory shortage, and shed about $263 billion in market value the same day Micron's blowout results sent chip shares climbing.
business_politics.summary: Micron Technology posted a 15-fold profit surge on AI-fueled demand for computer memory, driving a chipmaker rally, while Apple cited the same memory shortage for its first major price increases on MacBooks and iPads and absorbed one of the largest single-day market-capitalization losses on record.

sources.title: Apple increases MacBook and iPad prices by 20%
Micron posts 15-fold profit surge in boost for global AI stocks
U.S. Stocks Mixed as Micron Surges, Apple Slumps

business.title: Schwebel's to Close Local Outlet Stores
business.latest_developments: Schwebel's bakery outlet stores across western Pennsylvania will shut down as part of the company's wind-down, WTAE reported June 25.
business.summary: Schwebel Baking Company, the bread maker that announced its liquidation after more than 120 years, will close its Schwebel's retail outlet stores in the region, pulling a longtime brand from local shelves.

sources.title: Schwebel's Bakery Outlet stores to close

business.title: Pa. House Passes Data-Center Limits
business.latest_developments: The Pennsylvania House passed bills June 25 letting municipalities impose moratoriums on data-center projects and limiting their tax breaks, moving past the committee stage they cleared earlier.
business.summary: Responding to growing backlash over data centers' energy and water demands, the Pennsylvania House approved legislation allowing local governments to pause data-center development and curbing the tax incentives the projects receive.

sources.title: As data center backlash grows, bills allowing moratoriums, limiting tax breaks pass Pa. House

business.title: Downtown Tax-Diversion Plan Draws Fire
business.latest_developments: A plan to divert taxes from new Downtown construction into renovating the district drew objections from transit advocates over transparency and equity, PublicSource reported June 25.
business.summary: With Downtown Pittsburgh languishing in the post-pandemic shift to remote work, a proposal would channel tax revenue generated by new construction into funding district renovations, a plan transit advocates question on fairness grounds.

sources.title: Tax diversion plan to fund Downtown renovation roils transit advocates

around_town.title: Fayette Studies Penn State Campus Future
around_town.latest_developments: Fayette County leaders launched a privately funded feasibility study June 25 to find new uses for the Penn State Fayette campus near Uniontown, which closes after one more academic year.
around_town.summary: Penn State will shut its Fayette branch campus near Uniontown within a year, and County Commissioner Scott Dunn said the county wants to repurpose the site as an educational hub with multiple partners.

sources.title: Fayette County to study future opportunities at Penn State campus

around_town.title: Westmoreland 911 Seeks Rate Increase
around_town.latest_developments: Workers at the Westmoreland County 911 Center and the county commission pressed for an increase to the emergency surcharge, unchanged for more than three years, WTAE reported June 25.
around_town.summary: Staff at the Westmoreland County 911 Center say rising labor and technology costs have outstripped the surcharge that funds emergency dispatch, which has not risen in over three years.

sources.title: Westmoreland Commission, 911 workers plead for rate increase

around_town.title: Deluzio Opposes Veterans Benefits Bill
around_town.latest_developments: U.S. Representative Chris Deluzio of Allegheny County came out against the Take Care of America's Veterans Act, which he says would cut benefits, KDKA reported June 25.
around_town.summary: Congressman Chris Deluzio joined opposition to the Take Care of America's Veterans Act, arguing it would reduce care and benefits for disabled veterans like Allegheny County Army veteran Craig Romanovich.

sources.title: Deluzio joins opposition to bill he says would cut veterans' benefits

events.title: Pittsburgh Choreography Festival in Oakland
events.latest_developments: NEXTpittsburgh's weekend guide spotlights the Pittsburgh Dance Workshop and Choreography Festival, running through Saturday, June 27.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Dance Workshop and Choreography Festival runs Thursday, June 25, through Saturday, June 27, at the Charity Randall Theatre in Oakland, presenting original works by emerging and established choreographers from the region and beyond at various times.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from the Andy Warhol Bridge 100th Birthday Bash to OpenStreetsPGH

events.title: Nia Sioux in CLO's 'Mean Girls'
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 24 that former 'Dance Moms' star Nia Sioux will perform with Pittsburgh CLO at the Benedum Center.
events.summary: Nia Sioux, who rose to fame on 'Dance Moms,' returns to Pittsburgh to perform in the Pittsburgh CLO production of 'Mean Girls' at the Benedum Center, Downtown.

sources.title: 'Dance Moms' star Nia Sioux returns to Pittsburgh to perform at Benedum Center

around_teams.title: Horwitz to IL Amid Pirates Injuries
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette assessed June 25 how the injury-riddled Pirates fill the gap after first baseman Spencer Horwitz landed on the injured list.
around_teams.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates placed first baseman Spencer Horwitz on the injured list, deepening a run of injuries, as the beat weighs replacements and the rehab timeline for top prospect Konnor Griffin.

sources.title: With Spencer Horwitz on the IL, what's next for the injury-riddled Pirates?

around_teams.title: Hiles: Pirates Shouldn't Buy at Deadline
around_teams.latest_developments: Post-Gazette columnist Noah Hiles argued June 24 that the Pirates have not earned the right to add at the MLB trade deadline.
around_teams.summary: In a Post-Gazette column, Noah Hiles wrote that even with ace Paul Skenes, the Pirates' play under general manager Ben Cherington and owner Bob Nutting leaves them undeserving of buying at the deadline.

sources.title: Noah Hiles: These Pirates don't deserve to be buyers at the MLB trade deadline

around_teams.title: Steelers Film Study: Spears-Jennings
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette film study June 24 praised Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings for his effort and special-teams value.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette broke down Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings, drafted out of Oklahoma, highlighting his motor and likely early contribution on special teams.

sources.title: Steelers film study: Rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings brings effort, special teams value

team_usa.title: USMNT Rests Starters vs. Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: Already through as Group D winners, the United States faced Türkiye in its finale at Los Angeles Stadium with a heavily rotated lineup and Christian Pulisic left out.
team_usa.summary: Manager Mauricio Pochettino rotated his squad for the U.S. men's dead-rubber Group D match against Türkiye, sitting Christian Pulisic, as the Americans turn attention to the round of 32.

sources.title: Turkey v USA: World Cup 2026 – live
What Türkiye match will reveal about USMNT's knockout round hopes

team_usa.title: Hilary Knight Extends With PWHL Detroit
team_usa.latest_developments: Five-time U.S. Olympian Hilary Knight signed a two-year contract extension with PWHL Detroit on June 25, securing her through the 2028-29 season.
team_usa.summary: Hilary Knight, the veteran forward and five-time United States Olympian, extended her deal with her new PWHL expansion club in Detroit, keeping her under contract through 2028-29.

sources.title: Olympian Knight extends deal with PWHL Detroit

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the design company and why Field believes AI gives Figma a tailwind rather than a threat.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the tech industry mimics the surface rituals and language of past successes while chasing AI hype, imitating the form of winning companies without grasping what actually created their value.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport contends that AI firms publishing alarming warnings about the dangers of their own products amounts to a marketing tactic, as absurd as Ford issuing a whitepaper fretting that its F-150 is too powerful.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
